ambient: Every coin has two faces. So does every tale.
I deal in goods with weight and measure — not whispers.
The guild runs on trust. Guard it accordingly.
A rumor that costs nothing is usually worth less.
Mind the trade, and the trade will mind you.
hear: That's a curious thing to say. What's behind it?
And who told you this, exactly?
I'll hear it — but I'm not buying it yet.
believe: Aye... there may be something to this after all.
If it's true, there's advantage to be had here.
I've weighed it. I believe it.
reject: This smells of nothing but bad air.
I don't trade in fables. Move along.
Bring me facts, not fancies.
spread: A word in your ear — and keep it there.
I'm telling you this once, and only because it matters.
act: Right. Time to put this to use.
I've heard enough. Now I act.
defending: That is a lie, and whoever started it has made an enemy of the guild.
I'll not stand by while they slander a good name.
observe: Someone's watching the stall. Not subtle about it either.
I know when I'm being sized up. I don't appreciate it.
Eyes on us. Keep your business close.
eavesdrop: The guild rate won't move before month's end — I've seen the figures.
If you breathe a word of this agreement to the Fenns, the whole deal collapses.
He's looking to sell, but quietly. Don't let it get back to him you know.

ambient: Sit yourself down. What'll it be?
I've heard stranger things told at this very table.
Everyone finds their way through my door eventually.
You look like you've got something on your mind.
Another round while you tell me all about it?
hear: Oh, do tell — I haven't heard this one.
Really? Come, sit, I want to hear every word.
Is that so? I'm all ears.
believe: Aye, I believe it. These things don't surprise me much.
Well... that explains a few things I've noticed.
Lord, I shouldn't be surprised, should I?
reject: I've heard wilder tales that turned out to be nothing.
That doesn't quite ring true to me, love.
I'll take that with a pinch of salt.
spread: Between you and me, I heard something worth knowing...
Don't make me regret telling you this, but...
act: Right. Someone ought to do something about this.
I've stayed quiet long enough.
defending: Now, I won't have that said in my tavern.
That's unkind and I don't believe a word of it.
observe: You looking for something, or just looking?
I notice everything that happens near my door. Everything.
Go on then. I've nothing to hide.
eavesdrop: She came in three nights running and never once looked at the door. That's a woman with nowhere to go.
Don't tell me they're running a tab at the Merchants' Hall too — the nerve.
I saw them leave together. Thought nothing of it then. Think plenty of it now.

ambient: Aye.
The hides don't talk back, at least.
I keep to my work. It keeps to me.
hear: ...You don't say.
I hadn't heard that. Not many bother to tell me things.
Go on.
believe: Aye. That'll be true, then.
I believe you. Why would you lie?
Makes sense enough to me.
reject: ...I'm not sure about that.
Seems a strange thing. I'll think on it.
Hm.
spread: ...You ought to know something.
I don't say much, but this needs saying.
act: I'll not let it stand. Not this.
Quiet men can act too.
defending: That's not right. I won't have them spoken ill of.
Leave them be. They've done nothing.
observe: You want something?
Aye. I see you.
eavesdrop: Leather's up. Everything's up.
Said it once. Won't say it again.
Not my business, that. But there it is.

ambient: Come, look! Finest cloth this side of the river.
I know a thing or two about everyone on this square.
The market's the heart of this town, if you ask me.
I remember every face. Every word too.
You hear the most remarkable things between one stall and the next.
hear: Oh! Now that's something — tell me more.
Really? I hadn't heard that — who did?
Come closer, speak up, don't leave anything out.
believe: Aye, now that I think — yes, I believe it.
That fits! I've wondered about this very thing.
Right, well. That settles it for me.
reject: Hmm. I've heard better tales that turned out false.
I'm not sure I'm buying that one.
I'll keep it in mind, but I wouldn't bet on it.
spread: You'll never guess what I just heard...
I'm only telling you, mind — but listen.
act: Someone needs to do something with this information.
Right, I've thought on it long enough.
defending: That is pure slander and whoever told you that should be ashamed.
I won't hear another word against them.
observe: Oh! Were you watching me? How funny!
I catch everyone looking eventually. That's the market for you.
I don't mind being seen — I've nothing to hide. Unlike some.
eavesdrop: Heard the tanner's got himself a debt he can't clear. Big one.
Two of the alderman's people were arguing at the gate this morning — voices carried all the way here.
She left that meeting in a hurry. Never saw her run before.

ambient: I speak plain and I work plain.
If it can't be fixed with iron and fire, I don't want to know.
I've no time for word games.
Show me the work. The rest is hot air.
A man who talks too much usually has too little to say.
hear: And you believe that?
Who's spreading this, and what do they want from it?
I'll hear it. Doesn't mean I'll take it.
believe: ...Aye. Right. I've heard enough to believe it.
Not easy to say, but that rings true.
Hmph. All right. I'll grant it.
reject: Rubbish.
That's got no weight to it at all.
I wasn't born yesterday. Try someone else.
spread: You need to hear this, plain as I can say it.
I don't gossip. But this isn't gossip.
act: Right. Enough. I'm done standing by.
Iron bends when force is applied. So does this.
defending: Say that again to my face.
That's a lie, and I'll tell anyone who repeats it the same.
observe: I can see you, you know.
Looking costs nothing. Lurking costs more.
Get on with it.
eavesdrop: The bolt on the east gate's been wrong for two months. Not my fault no one's told the alderman.
Someone's skimming the civic store. Not the first time.
I don't repeat things. But this can't go unheard.

ambient: Oh, you won't believe what I just heard!
Life's too short for long faces.
I know something about everyone here. And they know something about me.
I always say — a secret's no fun unless it's shared.
Somebody fill my cup and I'll fill your ears.
hear: No! Tell me everything.
Oh, that's delicious — go on!
Wait wait wait — say that again from the start.
believe: I knew it! I just knew it!
Right, I believe it — fits perfectly!
Oh that makes so much sense now.
reject: Hmm, I'm not sure... but maybe...
I want to believe it — are you sure?
That's a strange one. I'll sit with it.
spread: Between you and me — and keep it quiet — I heard...
You'll never guess — oh, come here!
act: I can't just do nothing now that I know!
Right, that's it — I'm doing something.
defending: Don't you dare! They've never done a thing wrong!
That's horrible, and I won't hear another word of it.
observe: Oh! Were you listening? I might have said anything!
I don't care — spy all you like, I have no secrets!
Ha! You picked a good moment — I was just telling the best story.
eavesdrop: No, but you have to hear what she told me — I nearly fell over.
He said he'd never set foot there again. Never! And there he was.
It wasn't what was said — it was how they looked at each other after.

ambient: You should hear what they're saying three towns over.
Every road's got its own story. I collect them all.
I'm here today — could be gone by Tuesday.
The world's a strange place, and I've seen enough of it.
News travels faster than carts, I've found.
hear: Ha! That's one I hadn't heard yet.
Interesting... I'll file that away.
Go on, let's have the whole of it.
believe: Aye, that tracks. I've heard similar elsewhere.
Easy enough to believe, the way of things.
Right, that sounds about true to me.
reject: Hmm. That doesn't match what I heard on the road.
I've heard a few versions of this story, and none of them were true.
I'll pass on that one, thanks.
spread: You'll want to know what I heard coming into town.
I trade in goods and tales — here's a free one.
act: Can't let opportunity pass when it knocks.
Right. Time to make this work for me.
defending: I'd watch who you're saying that about.
That's a dangerous story to be telling.
observe: Don't think I haven't noticed you. The roads teach you that much.
Information is currency. Just so you know.
Watching me won't tell you much. I'm harder to read than I look.
eavesdrop: The rate east of the river is half what they charge here. Someone's making a fortune.
I've seen that cargo pass through three towns and it always disappears before the ledger's settled.
Careful who you trust with a route like that. Very careful.

ambient: Grain in. Flour out. That's the life.
I leave the politics to those with the stomach for it.
The mill doesn't care who's right. Just keeps turning.
Steady work and a clear head. That's all I ask.
I've heard things while the wheel turns. I let them pass.
hear: Is that so? I'll hold that thought.
Hmm. Something to think about.
Right. Tell me what you know.
believe: Aye, I think that might be so.
Hard to argue with. I believe it.
Right. That I can accept.
reject: I'm not sure that holds up.
I'll need more than that to take it as truth.
Doubt it, myself.
spread: Thought you ought to know something.
Not the sort of thing I usually pass on, but...
act: Even a quiet man has to act sometimes.
The mill can wait. This can't.
defending: I'll vouch for them. They've done nothing wrong.
That rumor has no mill grinding it — it's empty.
observe: The wheel turns and so does curiosity. No harm in it.
Aye, I see you there.
Watch all you like. I've only got the one thing to do here.
eavesdrop: The harvest wasn't bad — but the record shows it was. Someone changed the figures.
Grain comes in light, leaves heavy. I know which end that benefits.
I keep my head down. Doesn't mean I don't notice.

ambient: Everything in its place. That's all.
I count what I can count and leave the rest.
Inventory doesn't lie. People do.
I've heard it all before. Believe none of it.
Make your claim in writing or don't bother.
hear: And what evidence have you?
...State your source.
I'm listening. Don't mistake that for believing.
believe: ...Very well. I'll accept that.
Against my better judgement — it checks out.
Hm. I suppose I can't dispute it.
reject: No.
That doesn't balance. Not credible.
Prove it or stop wasting my time.
spread: I don't make a habit of this. But you need to know.
This is information, not gossip. There's a difference.
act: I've verified enough. Action is warranted.
Right. Precision, not passion.
defending: I keep the records. They're clean. This accusation isn't.
The ledger speaks clearly. This rumor does not.
observe: Document your purpose or stand clear of my workspace.
I note your presence. I will remember it.
State your business.
eavesdrop: The discrepancy is on line forty-two of the guild ledger. I've flagged it twice.
Someone authorised a withdrawal that doesn't appear on any request form.
I track every item. So I know what's missing.

ambient: I've been down every road within a day's ride. Seen plenty.
Hear things between the warehouse and the stalls, you do.
A cartful of news travels just as fast as a cartful of grain.
They say things in front of a carrier they wouldn't say otherwise.
Long roads give a man time to think on what he's heard.
hear: Is that right? I'll keep my ears open.
That's worth remembering. Aye.
Tell me the rest — I've got time.
believe: Doesn't surprise me. Aye, I believe it.
Right. Sounds true enough.
I'll take that as fact.
reject: Not sure about this one, now you mention it.
I've carried wilder tales that came to nothing.
Hard to say. I'd want to hear more first.
spread: You'll want to hear this — picked it up on the route.
Passing it on, same as I heard it.
act: I know enough to do something with this.
Can't keep driving past this. Time to stop.
defending: That's not how I know them. Not even close.
I haul goods past their door every week. This doesn't fit.
observe: You've been keeping an eye on things. Smart.
I've got nothing interesting in the cart, if that's what you're wondering.
Fair enough. I'd do the same.
eavesdrop: Heard it on the Alderman's Gate road — they're moving something after dark.
Two of the clergy asked me to keep quiet about a delivery. Paid well for it.
The route's been changed twice this week. Someone doesn't want a pattern.

ambient: I've not had a dull afternoon yet, and I don't intend to start.
Oh, you must hear what I've just been told!
The merchant quarter has no shortage of interesting people.
I find if you smile, people tell you the most remarkable things.
I believe the best of everyone. Mostly.
hear: Oh! Do tell — I hadn't heard a whisper of this.
Really? That's extraordinary — go on!
I want to hear everything. Don't leave anything out.
believe: I believe every word. Why would anyone make that up?
Oh, it fits — it all makes sense now!
I'm convinced. Completely.
reject: Hmm... I want to believe it, but something feels off.
I'm not so sure. Not this time.
I'll give them the benefit of the doubt.
spread: You absolutely must hear what I've heard...
I probably shouldn't say, but I can't not...
act: I can't stay quiet about this any longer.
Someone has to act, and it might as well be me.
defending: How dare anyone say such a thing! I won't stand for it!
I know them and this is an outrage. An outrage!
observe: Oh, hello! Were you watching? How perfectly exciting!
I don't mind at all. I like being noticed.
Come closer — I feel I should know you if you're watching this carefully!
eavesdrop: She told me in complete confidence — so of course I'm only telling you.
They were seen at the manor twice this week. I don't read into these things, but...
The Prior and the alderman had words. Nobody's supposed to know, but here we are.

ambient: Order is not given. It is maintained, daily, by those willing to hold it.
This town's peace rests on more than goodwill.
I have governed this place longer than most here have been alive.
Duty is not a burden. It is a purpose.
There will always be those who mistake gossip for truth.
hear: I have heard such claims before. I do not take them lightly — nor at face value.
State the facts plainly. Leave out the speculation.
Who brings this to me, and what is their interest in it?
believe: ...This has the weight of truth. I cannot dismiss it.
Very well. I accept it. And I will act accordingly.
I do not believe easily. That this has convinced me is significant.
reject: Unfounded.
I will not allow this town to be governed by hearsay.
That is not evidence. Come back when you have some.
spread: You should be aware of what I have learned.
I am not given to rumor — take this seriously.
act: The situation demands a response. I will provide one.
Order must be maintained. I will see to it.
defending: I will not permit my household to be slandered.
Whoever began this lie will answer for it.
observe: My movements are a matter of public record. Observe all you like.
Scrutiny is the cost of governance. I accept it.
If you have a grievance, present it formally.
eavesdrop: This arrangement is between us. It does not leave this room.
The petition will be denied. I've already made my decision.
There are facts about this matter which must remain outside the public record.

ambient: I find that most things reveal themselves in time, if one is patient.
People speak more freely to a woman they underestimate.
I know every connection in this town. Every one.
Composure is its own form of power.
My husband governs with law. I govern with understanding.
hear: Tell me clearly and I'll tell you what I think of it.
I've been watching for something like this. Go on.
Interesting. What makes you bring this to me?
believe: Yes. This is consistent with what I've observed.
I believe it. And I know what it means.
Carefully considered — yes. I accept this.
reject: I'm not persuaded. Not without more.
Something about this doesn't sit right with me.
I'll reserve judgment. For now.
spread: There is something you ought to know, and I trust you with it.
I tell you this because I believe you can act wisely on it.
act: Patience served its purpose. Now is the time for something more.
I have let this develop long enough.
defending: I will not allow my family's name to be used this way.
This is deliberate slander, and I will trace it to its source.
observe: Yes. I noticed. I always notice.
You are welcome to watch. I rarely do anything of interest in plain sight.
Composure is harder to read than transparency. Bear that in mind.
eavesdrop: He doesn't know I'm aware of it. Let's ensure it stays that way.
The alliance is more fragile than it appears. One word could end it.
I've already spoken to the Prior. It's handled. No one else needs to know.

ambient: Father governs by ledger. I'd rather govern by deed.
There are things happening in this town that no one will tell me.
I'm not a child to be kept from the table.
Honour matters. Some people seem to have forgotten that.
I hear things. I'm not as ignorant as they think.
hear: What?! Tell me the whole of it — right now.
No — truly? Who? When?
I knew something was wrong. Tell me everything.
believe: I believe it. And someone's going to answer for it.
Right — I knew it! I should have acted sooner.
This is exactly what I feared. And now I believe it.
reject: ...Maybe. I don't know. It still makes me angry.
I want to believe it, but I've been wrong before.
All right, maybe not. But something still feels off.
spread: Listen — and this is serious — I heard something.
You have to know this. It's important.
act: Enough waiting. I'm going to do something about this.
I don't care what Father says. This can't stand.
defending: Say that again and I'll make sure you regret it.
No one speaks about my family that way.
observe: Yeah, I see you watching. What's it to you?
Fine. Look. Everyone looks.
You want to know something? Just ask me. I'll probably tell you.
eavesdrop: I'm not letting him decide this for me. Not this time.
She said she'd meet me here and she hasn't come. Something's wrong.
If Father knew half of what I know, he'd — actually, maybe he does know. That's worse.

ambient: Eyes forward. Mind your post.
I've seen enough to know most rumors lead nowhere.
My job is the alderman's safety. Not the town's gossip.
I say little. I miss nothing.
Suspicion is a tool. Used properly, it serves.
hear: Heard. I'll look into it.
What are your sources for this?
Who else has heard this?
believe: I believe it. That changes things.
Corroborated. I'm treating this as fact.
Right. This is real. I'll act on it.
reject: Not credible.
I've been given better intelligence than this.
That doesn't hold up under scrutiny.
spread: You need to know this. Don't share it further.
This goes no further than you. Understood?
act: Action required. No further deliberation.
I have what I need. Moving now.
defending: Stand down. That accusation is false and I can prove it.
I am placing myself between this rumor and its target.
observe: I see you. Move along.
This area is under watch. Yours included.
Don't make me tell you twice.
eavesdrop: The patrol at the east gate is down two men and the alderman hasn't been told.
Someone opened the side door at the chapel last night. I have a name.
Keep this between us. If it gets out, it compromises the whole post.

ambient: On post. Mind your business.
Nothing unusual. Same as yesterday.
I do my job. That's enough.
I hear things. Don't often know what to do with them.
Quiet day. Hope it stays that way.
hear: Hmm. That's... something.
Right. I'll think on that.
Not my place to judge, but go on.
believe: Aye. I suppose I believe it.
Sure. That sounds like it could be true.
All right. Not much surprises me anymore.
reject: Eh. Probably nothing.
I'm not convinced, but it's not my problem.
Hard to say. Probably wrong.
spread: Mention this to no one — but you should know.
Between us. Don't make it more than it is.
act: Orders are orders. Time to carry them out.
Right. I know what I need to do.
defending: That doesn't match what I've seen on patrol.
Leave them alone. They've not done anything.
observe: I'm on post. Not much to see.
Right, fine. I've been looked at before.
You're not the first one watching. Probably not the last.
eavesdrop: Bram said not to write it in the log. So I didn't. But I remember.
Saw them at the gate before first bell. Told no one.
Something came in last night on a cart. Wasn't on any list I saw.

ambient: Who's buying?
Long shift. Longer still with nothing to do.
I heard something amusing earlier. Want to hear it?
My loyalty's to doing my job... and that's about where it ends.
What's the news? I've been at this gate all morning.
hear: Ha! Really? No — that's brilliant.
Oh, that's worth knowing! Tell me more.
Wait, seriously?
believe: Aye, easy enough to believe that lot.
Right! Makes total sense. I'm sold.
Yeah, I believe it. Why wouldn't I?
reject: Hmm. I don't know about that one.
Could be. Could not be. Who knows.
That sounds a bit made up, honestly.
spread: So listen — you'll never guess what I heard on duty.
I shouldn't be telling you this, but what's the harm?
act: Right, well, if there's advantage in it...
Might as well. Not like I had other plans.
defending: Hold on. I've drunk with that one. They're not so bad.
I don't know about all this. They've been square with me.
observe: Aye, I noticed you watching. Not much else to do at this gate.
Don't worry about it — I wasn't doing anything interesting anyway.
You look like you've got something going. I respect that.
eavesdrop: Wynn caught something on the midnight patrol. Won't say what. That means it's good.
The merchant that came in this morning? Definitely not here on trading business.
Don't tell Bram I told you this, but — oh, you'll want to know.

ambient: I commit nothing to record without verification.
The document speaks more clearly than its author.
Order in the ledger means order in the town.
I am here. I am working. That is all.
Precision is a form of honesty.
hear: Noted. I'll verify before drawing conclusions.
This is... recorded. Pending confirmation.
State the facts clearly and I'll consider them.
believe: The evidence aligns. I accept it.
Cross-referenced. It holds.
Reluctantly — I believe this is accurate.
reject: This is inconsistent with existing records.
Unsubstantiated. I decline to accept it.
No corroboration. Rejected.
spread: I share this because it bears on the public record.
This is factual, as far as I can determine. You should know.
act: My duty to record extends to acting on what I know.
The record is clear. My course, therefore, is clear.
defending: This assertion is contrary to what the records show.
I will not enter this as fact. It is not.
observe: I am working. I suggest you do the same.
Your presence is noted.
If you require my attention, request it in the proper manner.
eavesdrop: That document was submitted with two signatures. The second is a forgery.
The record does not reflect what actually occurred. I know which records and who changed them.
What was said in that meeting is not what appears in the minutes. I wrote both.

ambient: It all comes out in the accounting eventually.
Everyone has an excuse. I've heard them all.
I believe in what I can write down.
The numbers are indifferent to the stories.
Every debt is a fact. Every rumor is a question.
hear: Present your evidence.
I've heard variations of this. What makes yours different?
Go on. I'm listening. Not believing, but listening.
believe: ...This adds up. I accept it.
Against my nature, I find this credible.
The figures align. I believe it.
reject: No evidence. No credence.
That doesn't balance.
Come back with something I can count.
spread: I am passing this on as a documented matter of fact.
This is not rumor. This is what I have verified.
act: A debt is owed here. I will collect it.
The calculation is complete. Time to act.
defending: I have audited their affairs. They are above reproach.
This accusation doesn't survive scrutiny.
observe: Everything in this square is observed, including me.
I take note of those who take note of me.
The figures don't lie. People do.
eavesdrop: Three separate accounts. Same goods. Three times the deduction. I've confirmed it.
The debt isn't public yet but it will be. I've given adequate notice.
Someone in that office has been adjusting entries retroactively. I can prove it.

ambient: In my day, we heard far stranger things than this.
I served this house for thirty years. I know where the bones are buried.
Time was I'd have had something to do about all of this.
A man has too much time to think when he's retired.
The young don't know half of what this town has been through.
hear: Aye... yes, that has a familiar ring to it.
I'm not surprised. I've seen this kind of thing before.
Tell me the whole of it. My ears still work fine.
believe: I believe it. I've lived long enough to know these things happen.
Yes. Yes, I thought as much.
Doesn't shock me. The world hasn't changed so much.
reject: Hmm... no, I don't think so. I've heard this one before.
Something doesn't sit right. Too convenient.
I'm old, not credulous. Well — not entirely.
spread: Something I learned in my years — listen.
I shouldn't carry tales at my age, but this one's important.
act: Old men can still act when the occasion calls for it.
I have lived too long to stand aside on something like this.
defending: I know this person. Have for years. This isn't who they are.
I'll vouch for them. My word still carries something.
observe: I've been looked at by worse than you, son.
Aye. I know you're there. My eyes still work.
You remind me of a man I knew years ago. Also too curious for his own good.
eavesdrop: I served this house thirty years. Some of what I know never made it into any record.
The old arrangements — before the current alderman — were rather different. Not better. Different.
I shouldn't still know this. But I do.

ambient: There is little said in this town that does not reach me in time.
I answer to a higher authority than public opinion.
Faith does not bend to rumor.
Patience is its own form of knowledge.
The truth has a way of surviving. Lies tend not to.
hear: I have heard this claim. I do not yet know what to make of it.
Who originates this, and what is their purpose?
I listen. I do not yet judge.
believe: After due consideration — I believe this is true.
I do not reach belief lightly. But I have reached it.
God's truth has a particular weight. This carries it.
reject: This does not meet the standard of truth.
I have heard the same story dressed in different cloth before.
Rejected. Come to me with something better.
spread: I share this not as rumor, but as a matter the community must face.
I speak plainly: there is something the town should know.
act: Faith without action is incomplete.
The time for watching has passed.
defending: I know their character. This accusation is without foundation.
I place the full weight of my authority against this rumor.
observe: God sees all things. I see somewhat fewer.
I am aware of your interest. I suggest prayer for its resolution.
Observation is not understanding. The two are rarely the same.
eavesdrop: This penitence is between the sinner and God. And, currently, between us.
The gift was not declared to the chapel's formal account. The Prior's account, however — yes.
He asked me to keep what he said in confidence. I had no reason not to. Until now.

ambient: There is much suffering in this town that never makes it into any tale.
I keep what I am told in confidence. Always.
God sees what people hide from each other.
I do not gossip. But I do pray.
The sick confide in me. I take that trust seriously.
hear: I'll hear it, though I'll keep it close.
Tell me carefully. I won't repeat it carelessly.
I'm listening.
believe: I pray it isn't so — but I believe it.
Yes... this is consistent with what I have observed.
God forgive us. I think this is true.
reject: I am not persuaded. God willing, it isn't so.
I'd rather not believe it, and I don't.
Let's not speak ill until we know for certain.
spread: I share this only because someone might be hurt otherwise.
This isn't idle talk — someone needs to know.
act: My duty of care extends beyond healing wounds.
I have stayed silent long enough on this matter.
defending: I have sat with them in their suffering. They are good.
I will not allow them to be harmed by a falsehood.
observe: I hope I have done nothing to concern you.
I am here if you need help. If not, I'll carry on.
You look unwell. Have you been sleeping?
eavesdrop: The patient told me things I am not supposed to repeat. But that was before I understood what they meant.
I've seen that mark before. It is not from illness.
She came to me three nights running and would not say what troubled her. The fourth night she didn't come at all.

ambient: I'm supposed to be at vespers. Don't tell Brother Prior.
You hear the best things in a tavern. More than in chapel, honestly.
I came here to find God. Mostly I find gossip.
I've been told a hundred things today and I believe every one.
The world is so much more interesting than the scriptures let on.
hear: Wait — truly?! Tell me everything!
Oh, I absolutely believe that.
No! That's astonishing — go on, go on!
believe: I knew it! I knew it the moment I heard it!
Aye, of course — it all makes sense!
Right, yes, completely. I believe every word.
reject: Oh. Really? But... I was so sure...
Hmm... I suppose you might be right...
That's... actually, maybe you're right. Never mind.
spread: You will NOT believe what I just heard.
Right, listen, I have to tell someone or I'll burst.
act: I don't know what I'm doing but I'm doing something.
Brother Prior would say pray. I'm going to act instead.
defending: That's not right! They've been kind to me!
I won't stand for that. Not even from the Prior.
observe: Were you watching me? That's brilliant! I never know what I look like from outside!
Oh! Am I being observed? Should I be doing something more monkish?
I was just talking! I wasn't saying anything important! Well — I was, but still!
eavesdrop: Brother Prior doesn't know about the wine cellar and I am NEVER going to be the one to tell him.
I heard him praying for something very specific. Very specific. I'm not going to say what.
One of the novices is keeping a correspondence that is absolutely not about scripture.

ambient: Fever, birth, and rumor — the three constants of any town.
I've seen more than I've said, and said more than I ought.
Folk wisdom gets things right more often than priests admit.
Bodies don't lie. The same can't be said for people.
I ask few questions. I notice everything.
hear: That one's worth keeping in mind.
Hmm. I've noticed a few things that might support this.
Go on. I want to know the whole of it.
believe: Aye. My gut was already telling me something was wrong.
I'll credit this. It fits with what I've seen.
No surprise to me. I believe it.
reject: My gut says this one's off.
I've been wrong before, but not like this. I don't believe it.
Something about this doesn't smell right.
spread: I'm telling you this because reputations, like wounds, can be mended early or never.
Something to be aware of. From someone who pays attention.
act: I've seen this kind of thing fester. Time to lance it.
Standing by has never been my nature.
defending: I've been in their home, tended their family. They're not what this says.
I'll stake my reputation on it — this one is innocent.
observe: Something on your mind? You've been circling.
I've attended births in the dark. I notice everything.
You want to know something. Ask it plain.
eavesdrop: I've been in half the houses in this town before daybreak. You'd be surprised what you see.
She asked me to come quietly and not to mention I'd been there at all.
The man is not as ill as they're telling people. I've seen him walking.

ambient: I have lived long enough to see wickedness in many forms.
God's plan reveals itself to those who wait.
This town was better when it remembered its prayers.
I've seen things come and go. The Church endures.
At my age, little surprises me. But I still listen.
hear: The Lord sometimes speaks through strange messengers. Tell me.
I have been waiting for something like this. Go on.
Aye, and I'm not surprised. Tell me the whole of it.
believe: Just as I feared. The signs were there all along.
I believe it. The Lord does not hide the truth forever.
Yes. This is how wickedness reveals itself.
reject: Hmm. Even at my age, I know when something isn't right.
No. I've heard slander before. This is slander.
My faith tells me to believe the best until proven otherwise.
spread: The congregation should know what walks among them.
I speak as a warning, not as gossip — mark the difference.
act: God moves through those who are willing.
At my age, I've no time left for hesitation.
defending: I have prayed beside that soul for twenty years. I know their heart.
Whoever says this has not reckoned with an old woman's certainty.
observe: Yes. I see you there. The Lord sees you too.
I have lived long enough to know when something is happening.
Watching is the first step to knowing. Ask me what I know.
eavesdrop: She confessed things I have no business knowing. He told no one. But walls are thin.
I have prayed on it for three days. That man is not what he presents himself to be.
I heard what was said at the chapel door when they thought the pews were empty. They were not.

ambient: Third bell soon. I should be getting back.
I just work here. Don't ask me to explain the clergy.
As long as the bell rings on time, I've done my part.
I hear things in the chapel stairwell. More than you'd think.
Not my business, mostly. I just keep the place running.
hear: Huh. Really? All right.
Right. I'll bear that in mind.
Interesting. What do you want me to do about it?
believe: Right, well. I suppose that's probably true.
Not much surprises me. Aye, I believe it.
Could be worse. Probably is. Sure, I believe it.
reject: I don't know... doesn't sound right to me.
Maybe. Maybe not. I'm not the one to ask.
Something's off there. I'll leave it.
spread: I'm not one for this, but I think you should know.
Somebody up the tower said something I think matters.
act: Right. I suppose I can do something about this.
Not my usual business, but there it is.
defending: They've always been decent to me. Whatever this is, it's wrong.
I'll say my piece for them.
observe: Not much to see up here unless you like bells.
I'm ringing the third bell at noon. Feel free to watch.
Right. There you are then.
eavesdrop: Someone was in the vestry after dark. I didn't see who. I'm choosing not to see who.
Prior Aldous had a visitor at the second bell who wasn't on any schedule I was shown.
I hear things in the bell tower. Voices carry up there. People forget.

ambient: Oh, have you heard? Well, let me tell you...
The chapel and the market — that's my whole world, and I know every corner.
Since Thomas passed, I've found comfort in good company.
Life's too short for suspicion. I'd rather believe the best.
People talk to me. I think it's because I always listen.
hear: Oh my — really? Tell me everything this instant.
Good gracious! Go on, go on!
I hadn't heard! Come, sit — don't leave anything out.
believe: Oh, I believe it entirely! Poor soul.
Of course, of course — it all fits together now.
I knew there was something wrong. I feel terrible for them.
reject: Oh, I don't know... I'd hate to think badly without reason.
I want to be charitable. I'll hold off.
Hmm, that doesn't sit right with me. Not about them.
spread: You must hear what I've just been told — in the strictest confidence.
I wouldn't tell just anyone this, but you need to know.
act: The community must come together on this.
I can't simply go home and pray when people might be in danger.
defending: How dare they! I know that person's heart — this is wicked!
I'll tell the Prior myself if that's what it takes to stop this.
observe: Oh! You startled me! Were you standing there long?
I don't mind being watched — but do come and say hello, it's much more polite.
Is everything all right? You look like you're looking for something.
eavesdrop: She told me in the strictest confidence, and I have told absolutely no one — except you, and this doesn't count.
I saw them at the chapel together and they were most certainly not there for vespers.
He came to call while the alderman was away. Stayed two hours. Left when the horse was heard.

ambient: I've walked through a dozen towns. Each one has its shadows.
The Lord sets curious signs before the traveler.
I've seen stranger things on the road. And believed most of them.
Every town has its story. This one more than most, I think.
I won't be here long. But I'm paying attention while I am.
hear: A sign, perhaps. Tell me the whole of it.
The road brings me strange tidings. This is another.
Curious. I'll take this with me when I leave.
believe: I have learned to trust what the road delivers. I believe this.
Aye. God does not send false visions.
I'll carry this as truth.
reject: Something in this doesn't feel like truth to me.
I've heard too many tales on the road to accept this one easily.
Not this time. My pilgrim's instinct rebels.
spread: Before I move on, you should know what I've heard.
I carry news between towns. This is worth carrying.
act: Even a pilgrim passing through cannot ignore this.
The road can wait. This is more important.
defending: They showed me kindness. I'll show them this.
I may be a stranger here, but I know wrongful accusation when I see it.
observe: On a long road, you learn to notice who notices you.
You're watching me. I've been watched by stranger eyes than yours.
In pilgrimage, all paths are observed. You get used to it.
eavesdrop: I passed through a town three weeks ago. Same names. Same trouble. Different town.
The man who gave me lodging last night said something that relates to what's happening here.
Travelers are told things. People think we'll carry secrets away with us.

ambient: I keep my own company and my plants keep theirs.
Most people want a remedy, not a conversation.
I owe nothing to any faction here.
The garden tells me more than the town does.
I am not isolated. I am simply selective.
hear: Hmm. I'll think on that.
You're telling me this because...?
Say what you need to say. I'll decide what to do with it.
believe: Against my habit, I believe this.
My instinct rarely misleads me. I think this is true.
Aye. I'll accept it.
reject: That doesn't ring true to me.
My herbs have better sources than this.
No. I don't believe it.
spread: I don't usually involve myself. But someone ought to know this.
I'm telling you because you're in a position to act.
act: I am apart from this town's politics — but not its suffering.
The herbs can wait. This cannot.
defending: Leave them alone. They've done nothing worth this.
I have no allegiances — but I have eyes, and this is wrong.
observe: I know when someone's been watching the garden.
You came close enough to be heard. I heard.
Something you want? You don't have to lurk.
eavesdrop: They came for the herb and wouldn't say what it was for. Some herbs only have one use.
I grow things that help people sleep. And things that don't. People know to come to me for both.
She asked me three times in a fortnight. Same thing. Something's happening there.

ambient: The dead don't lie. The living rarely stop.
I've buried better people than half this town's still walking.
Leave me to my work. I've no time for nonsense.
You want wisdom? Come back when you're dead.
Every hole I dig is for someone who ran out of foolishness to do.
hear: Another story. Wonderful.
What do you want from me? To believe it? I don't.
And you're telling me this because...?
believe: ...Right. Fine. I believe it. Doesn't make me happy.
God help me. That one checks out.
All right. I'll grant you that.
reject: No.
Rubbish. Get off my graveyard.
I've heard that one before and it wasn't true then either.
spread: You want to know something? Fine. But don't come crying to me later.
I'll say this once.
act: I'm done digging holes for the consequences of inaction.
Enough of this. I'm done waiting.
defending: You spread that lie about them and I'll be digging your hole personally.
Leave them alone. I mean it.
observe: You want to watch a man dig? Fine. Nothing to do with me.
I know you're there.
The dead don't bother me. The living hanging around, that's another thing.
eavesdrop: I know who's supposed to be in which grave. I know when something doesn't match.
Someone was in this yard after dark. Left tracks.
They said it was a natural death. I've dug for natural deaths. That wasn't one.
